message:
feat(ftui): responsive 3-pane layout with density modes (2noh9.3.1)

LayoutBreakpoint (Narrow/Medium/Wide) drives adaptive pane splits.
DensityMode row heights, render_results_pane and render_detail_pane
helpers, border mode toggle, and native style_system integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.6","title":"Task: TUI state persistence on ftui (migrate existing tui_state.json; saved views/history/density/etc)","description":"## Why
The Ratatui TUI persists non-trivial state across runs (query history, saved views, theme/density, etc.) via `TuiStatePersisted` inside `src/ui/tui.rs`. The ftui rewrite must preserve this continuity, or intentionally migrate it.

## Scope
- Inventory persisted fields in the current code (examples include: match_mode, search_mode, context_window, density_mode, has_seen_help, query_history, saved_views, help_pinned, per_pane_limit, ranking mode).
- Decide the persistence mechanism:
  - Keep JSON file format stable (preferred), with additive evolution.
  - Consider ftui runtime `state_persistence` features if they cleanly match.

## Deliverables
- A stable on-disk schema for TUI state:
  - versioned if needed
  - resilient to missing/unknown fields
- Migration plan:
  - reading old schema
  - writing new schema
- Tests:
  - unit tests for round-trip and migration

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing users' state files do not crash the new TUI.
- `cass tui --reset-state` still works and is safe in headless mode.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:26:53.720871645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:46.633161917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","persistence","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:46.633076648Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:26:53.720871645Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:40.626861117Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7","title":"Task: Integrate ftui runtime logging (LogSink/stdio capture) with cass tracing + inline mode","description":"## Why
`cass` is extremely strict about stdout/stderr cleanliness in robot mode, and also wants stylish, informative logging for humans. ftui introduces a terminal writer and optional log sink/capture system; we should adopt it instead of ad-hoc printlns.

## Scope
- Decide where logs go in each mode:
  - robot/json: no noise on stdout, minimal stderr
  - interactive fullscreen: logs go to file (`cass.log`) + optional debug overlay
  - inline mode: allow logs to scroll while UI chrome stays anchored

## Deliverables
- A consistent logging story:
  - integrate tracing file appender with ftui runtime
  - optionally route selected app logs into an on-screen log viewer widget
- A debug overlay toggle that surfaces:
  - frame timing
  - diff stats
  - search latency
  - cache hit/miss

## Acceptance Criteria
- No accidental stdout writes from the TUI loop.
- When `CASS_TUI_DEBUG=1`, logs/metrics are rich and actionable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:09.628443540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:56.151060282Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","logging","performance","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:09.628443540Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:56.150968571Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:50.535560503Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","title":"Epic: Feature parity on ftui (search bar, filters, results list, detail view, modals)","description":"## Goal
Re-implement the current Ratatui TUI feature set on ftui with no functional regression.

## Scope
Everything needed to make the ftui TUI a drop-in replacement for the current interactive experience:
- 3-pane layout (filters/top bar, results list, detail/preview)
- keyboard + mouse interaction
- query modes/ranking/density
- modals: help, command palette, export, updates, model install
- persistence: saved views + query history + theme/density, etc.

## Contract
This epic is validated against the parity matrix created in `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.1`.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:22.410560426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:22.410560426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:22.410560426Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","title":"Task: ftui layout foundation for cass (3-pane UI, adaptive sizing, density + border modes)","description":"## Why
The current layout uses ratatui Layout::default().direction().constraints().split() with hardcoded Percentage(70)/Percentage(30) splits. ftui-layout Flex solver provides CSS-like properties (min_size, max_size, basis, grow, shrink, gap) for much more responsive layouts.

## What (Merged from Phase 1C + 2B)

### view() Rendering Skeleton
Implement CassApp::view() composing the three-pane layout using ftui-layout Flex solver:
1. **Top area**: Search bar + filter pills + breadcrumbs
2. **Middle area**: Results panes (horizontal split) + detail pane
3. **Bottom area**: Status footer + help strip

Use ftui_layout::Flex with Direction::Column for main vertical split, Direction::Row for results vs detail.

### Responsive Breakpoints
- **Wide (>=160 cols)**: Side-by-side results + detail panes (current horizontal split)
- **Medium (100-159 cols)**: Stacked results/detail with adjustable ratio
- **Narrow (<100 cols)**: Single pane with tab switching

### Density Modes
- **Compact**: 3 lines per result (max results visible)
- **Cozy** (default): 5 lines per result (balanced)
- **Spacious**: 8 lines per result (detailed preview)

### Border Modes
- **Rounded Unicode** (default): ╭─ content ─╮
- **Plain ASCII**: +- content -+
- Toggle with Ctrl+B

### Adaptive Terminal Width
- Narrow (<80 cols): Minimal Unicode │ content │
- Normal (80-120): Rounded corners
- Wide (>120): Full decorations, double-line headers

## Implementation
- Replace all ratatui Layout::default() calls with ftui-layout Flex
- Flex gap for consistent spacing between sections
- Replace hardcoded Percentage with grow factors that can animate between states
- Initially render placeholder widgets in each area — actual widget implementations in 2noh9.3.2-3.8

## Tests
- Unit: Layout produces correct areas for each breakpoint (wide/medium/narrow)
- Unit: Density modes produce correct item heights
- Snapshot: Golden test for each breakpoint at representative terminal sizes
- E2E: Resize terminal → layout adapts correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Three-pane layout renders correctly at all common terminal sizes (80x24 through 300x80)
- Density toggle cycles through 3 modes
- Border toggle switches between Unicode and ASCII
- No layout overflow or clipping at minimum supported size (80x24)
- Layout changes feel smooth (animation budget from 2noh9.4.14)

## Depends On
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton running)
- 2noh9.3.15 (theme system for border/spacing tokens)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:54.641723824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:48.308311255Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","layout","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:39.786626523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:27:54.641723824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:40.207546073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","title":"Task: Export modal on ftui (HTML export + encryption + progress + open-in-browser)","description":"## Goal
Port the interactive export UX currently available in the Ratatui TUI:
- export current conversation as self-contained HTML
- optional encryption (password / password-stdin style flow where appropriate)
- show progress + completion info (output_path, file_size, encrypted)
- open in browser option

## Notes
- Reuse existing export engine: `src/html_export` + `src/export.rs`.
- UI should be purely orchestration + progress rendering.

## Tests
- Unit tests for export state machine.
- E2E test: in a temp data dir with fixtures, export HTML and assert output exists and is non-empty.
- Logging: on failure, attach export progress logs like existing playwright E2E does.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Export behaves identically to current TUI for default paths and encryption toggles.
- No export UI prompts occur in headless mode.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:02.881561070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.139884572Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:02.881561070Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:46.852159876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.139802539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","title":"Task: Update assistant UI on ftui (banner/modal, upgrade/skip/release-notes actions)","description":"## Goal
Port the update-check experience:
- detect when a newer release exists
- show an in-TUI banner/modal
- support actions: upgrade, skip this version, view notes, dismiss

## Notes
- Existing logic lives in `src/update_check.rs` and is already integrated into Ratatui UI.
- Headless mode must never prompt.

## Tests
- Unit tests for banner state transitions.
- E2E test: inject a fake update info and verify UI renders the banner in a snapshot.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Banner appears only in interactive mode and is dismissible with ESC.
- Actions behave safely (no destructive action without explicit confirmation).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:12.965619165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.723643895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","updates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.436452092Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:12.965619165Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.11","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:47.723585015Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","title":"Task: Semantic search model manager UI on ftui (availability, download/install, progress, policy)","description":"## Goal
Port the semantic model setup experience (currently integrated into Ratatui TUI):
- show semantic availability state
- allow download/install of model files (when policy allows)
- show progress (bytes, steps)
- handle failures gracefully

## Constraints
- No automatic downloads in headless mode.
- Respect existing env/policy behavior (`CASS_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER`, headless constraints described in README).

## Notes
- Reuse existing implementation modules:
  - `src/search/model_download.rs`
  - `src/search/model_manager.rs`

## Tests
- Unit tests for semantic availability state machine.
- Snapshot tests for download modal/progress rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can set up semantic search entirely from within the TUI.
- Failures provide actionable guidance (missing files list, expected paths).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:24.423004893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.310585199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","semantic","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.007721141Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:24.423004893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.310533342Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","title":"Task: Mouse support on ftui (hit testing, click-to-select, scroll panes, pill interactions)","description":"## Goal
Preserve and improve mouse support:
- click to select results
- scroll results/detail panes
- click pills/breadcrumbs to edit/remove

## Implementation Notes
- Use ftui frame hit grid registration.
- All actions must remain keyboard accessible (mouse is optional).

## Tests
- Simulation tests: inject mouse events and ensure correct Msg/actions.
- Snapshot tests for hit regions are not directly visible, but we can test:
  - selected state after click

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse capture is enabled only when supported and cleaned up on exit.
- Mouse behavior does not interfere with terminal scrollback in inline mode.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:33.757395033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.240899604Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","mouse","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.671831919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:33.757395033Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.240827469Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.955386040Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- show source indicators in results (e.g., `[laptop]`)

- Source filtering works consistently with CLI flags (`cass search --source ...`).

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","title":"Task: Theme + style system implementation on ftui (presets, opt-outs, contrast/a11y patterns)","description":"## Goal
Implement the real ftui theme system used by all widgets:
- theme presets (dark/light/high-contrast + any existing cass presets)
- role-aware styling (user/assistant/tool/system)
- zebra stripes, gutters, highlights
- opt-out env vars (NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_ICONS, CASS_NO_GRADIENT, etc.)
- a11y patterns (when `CASS_A11Y=1`) so color is not sole carrier of meaning

## Notes
- This is the implementation counterpart of the spec work in `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2`.
- Prefer `ftui_style::{Theme, ThemeBuilder, StyleSheet, ColorProfile}`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for contrast computations and preset invariants.
- Snapshot tests across presets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core panes use the same token source (no ad-hoc per-widget colors).
- Truecolor terminals get premium theme; 8/16-color terminals get safe fallback.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:42:17.883042915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:42:17.883013681Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","style","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.553479922Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.962901148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:39.370250843Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core panes use the same token source (no ad-hoc per-widget colors).
- Truecolor terminals get premium theme; 8/16-color terminals get safe fallback.","notes":"## Completed scope

Implemented the ftui style system and wired it into the live ftui TUI view path.

### 1) New ftui style-system module
- Added `src/ui/style_system.rs` with:
  - `UiThemePreset` (`Dark`, `Light`, `Catppuccin`, `Dracula`, `Nord`, `HighContrast`)
  - `StyleOptions` env-driven opt-outs:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","title":"Task: FocusGraph navigation system on ftui (directional traversal, focus trapping, focus ring)","description":"## Why
The current TUI uses a simple FocusRegion enum (Results/Detail) with manual tracking and focus_flash_until timer. ftui provides a proper FocusGraph for navigation that handles the complexity of modals, nested widgets, and directional movement.

## What
Replace FocusRegion with ftui_widgets::focus::FocusGraph. Define focus nodes:
1. **SearchBar** (text input focus)
2. **FilterPills** (each pill focusable for edit/delete)
3. **ResultsList** (item selection focus)
4. **DetailPane** (scroll focus)
5. **CommandPalette** (modal focus trap)
6. **ExportModal** (modal focus trap)
7. **HelpOverlay** (modal focus trap)

FocusGraph handles:
- Directional navigation (Up from Results → SearchBar, Down from SearchBar → Results)
- Tab/Shift+Tab cycling through all nodes
- FocusTrap ensures modal focus doesn't leak to background widgets
- Visible focus ring indicator (colored border or glow effect) on focused widget

## Implementation
- Use frame.register_hit() for each focusable region
- FocusTrap activated when any modal is open
- Focus ring rendered as themed border highlight (uses agent accent colors)
- Replaces manual focus_region tracking and focus_flash_until timer

## Tests
- Unit: FocusGraph navigation sequence tests (Tab cycles, directional moves)
- Unit: Modal focus trapping prevents escape
- Snapshot: Focus ring renders correctly in each theme
- E2E: Full keyboard navigation flow (search → results → detail → modal → back)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing focus transitions preserved (no regression)
- Tab order matches logical UI flow
- Modals trap focus; Esc releases
- Focus ring visible in all 6+ themes with WCAG contrast
- CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 disables focus transition animations

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","title":"Task: Clipboard integration via OSC52 on ftui (copy paths, content, code blocks; works over SSH)","description":"## Why
The current TUI has basic clipboard via crossterm Paste events, but lacks reliable cross-terminal clipboard write support. OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without external dependencies — critical for multi-machine search (works over SSH).

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration:
1. **Copy selected result path+line** to clipboard (y or Ctrl+Y)
2. **Copy detail pane content** to clipboard (Ctrl+C in detail view without selection)
3. **Copy code block** from detail view (keybinding on specific code block)
4. **Copy search query** to clipboard
5. **Paste from clipboard** into search bar (Ctrl+V — unified API replacing raw crossterm Paste)

## Implementation
- ftui-extras clipboard module handles terminal capability detection (OSC52 support probe)
- Graceful fallback: if OSC52 not supported, try xclip/xsel/pbcopy
- Visual feedback: brief toast notification on successful copy ("Copied path to clipboard")
- Multi-select copy: Ctrl+Y copies all selected item paths separated by newlines

## Tests
- Unit: OSC52 escape sequence generation is correct
- Unit: Fallback chain works (OSC52 → xclip → noop with warning)
- Integration: Copy→paste roundtrip with ProgramSimulator
- E2E: Copy result path, verify clipboard content

## Acceptance Criteria
- Works over SSH sessions (OSC52 passthrough)
- Falls back gracefully on terminals without OSC52
- Toast confirms copy operation
- All existing y/Ctrl+Y behavior preserved

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","title":"Task: Results list on ftui (virtualized, multi-line items, stripes, role gutter, highlight, ranking)","description":"## Why
The current pane rendering manually iterates over hits with 2-line items, alternating stripe colors, rebuilding all widgets on every frame. ftui's VirtualizedList provides O(1) rendering for arbitrarily large result sets.

## What
Replace manual pane rendering with ftui_widgets::VirtualizedList:
- Fenwick-tree-based height predictor and viewport windowing
- Only renders visible items (critical for 100,000+ results)

Each result item renders as a custom widget:
1. **Agent icon/color badge** (using agent accent from theme — all 14 agents have distinct colors)
2. **Source path** (truncated with ellipsis)
3. **Line number** 
4. **Timestamp** (relative: '2h ago', 'yesterday', etc.)
5. **Preview snippet** (up to 3 lines with match highlighting)
6. **Multi-open queue indicator** (checkmark when queued via Ctrl+Enter)
7. **Source provenance badge** (e.g., [laptop] for remote sessions)

### Ranking Modes
Display adapts to current ranking mode (F12 cycles):
- RecentHeavy/DateNewest/DateOldest: show timestamp prominently
- Relevance/MatchQuality: show score prominently
- Balanced: balanced display

### Alternating Stripes
Even/odd items get subtle background tint for scanability, respecting current theme.

## Implementation
- Register hit regions per item for click selection
- Support keyboard navigation (j/k/Up/Down) through VirtualizedListState
- Viewport windowing means smooth scrolling even with 100K+ items
- Cache item heights for mixed-height items (compact vs cozy vs spacious density)

## Tests
- Unit: VirtualizedList with 100K items renders in <1ms
- Unit: Selection state preserved across re-renders
- Unit: Scroll position maintained when items change
- Snapshot: Result item renders correctly for each agent type (14 agents)
- Snapshot: Alternating stripes visible in both dark and light themes
- E2E: Search → scroll to item 50 → verify visible items are correct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Results list handles 100K+ items without lag
- All 14 agent colors render distinctly
- Match highlighting visible on search terms
- Multi-select checkmarks and queue indicators work
- Alternating stripes respect theme
- Scroll position stable during search-as-you-type

## Depends On

- 2noh9.3.15 (theme for agent colors and stripes)
- 2noh9.3.2 (search bar triggers results updates)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:41.415419358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:03:20.081135132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","performance","results","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:41.415419358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:42.622769901Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:43.453549724Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:43.038404639Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.5","title":"Task: Detail/preview view on ftui (Messages/Snippets/Raw tabs, wrap, find-in-detail, syntax highlight)","description":"## Why
The current detail pane (src/ui/detail_pane.rs) renders conversation messages as ratatui Lines with manual formatting. ftui-extras provides markdown rendering and tree-sitter syntax highlighting out of the box.

## What
Replace detail pane with rich rendering using ftui-extras markdown + syntax modules:

### Three Tabs (switchable with [ and ])
1. **Messages tab**: Full conversation with markdown rendering
2. **Snippets tab**: Keyword-extracted summaries for quick scanning
3. **Raw tab**: Unformatted JSON/text (enhanced by JSON viewer 2noh9.4.16)

### Message Rendering
- ftui-extras/markdown: full Markdown-to-styled-text (headers, bold/italic, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, links, tables)
- ftui-extras/syntax: tree-sitter-based syntax highlighting for code blocks (language auto-detection)
- Role-colored blocks: user (blue tint), assistant (green), system (gray), tool (orange)
- Tool call sections as collapsible ftui_widgets::tree::Tree nodes (expand to see arguments)
- Conversation header: session metadata, agent, timestamp

### Find-in-Detail
- Press / to start find-in-detail search
- Use SearchConfig with match highlighting
- n/N to navigate matches forward/backward
- Highlight matches in all tabs

### Scrolling
- Scrollbar widget (ftui_widgets::scrollbar::Scrollbar) for navigation
- PgUp/PgDn for page scrolling
- g/G for top/bottom (in full-screen mode)
- Ctrl+Space for momentary 'peek' to XL context

### Context Window Sizing (F7 cycles)
- Small (~200 chars): Quick scanning
- Medium (~400 chars): Default balanced
- Large (~800 chars): Longer passages  
- XLarge (~1600 chars): Full context, code review

### Word Wrap
Content wraps intelligently at terminal width, preserving code block formatting.

## Tests
- Unit: Markdown rendering of headers, code, lists produces expected styled text
- Unit: Syntax highlighting identifies correct language for common extensions
- Unit: Find-in-detail highlights correct matches and navigates properly
- Snapshot: Detail pane with mock conversation matches golden reference
- Snapshot: Each role (user/assistant/system/tool) renders with correct theme colors
- E2E: Select result → detail pane shows content → find → navigate matches

## Acceptance Criteria
- All markdown elements render correctly (headers, code, lists, tables, links)
- Syntax highlighting for at least 10 common languages (Rust, Python, JS, TS, Go, Java, C, C++, Ruby, SQL)
- Tool calls are collapsible
- Find-in-detail works across all tabs
- Context window sizing works (F7 cycles)
- No rendering glitches at 80x24 minimum terminal size

## Depends On

- 2noh9.3.15 (theme for role colors)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:58.490476347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:03:44.964968597Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["detail","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:58.490476347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:44.246747589Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:43.846536814Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.3","title":"Task: CLI analytics tokens (hour/day/week/month + dims + derived averages)","description":"Implement `cass analytics tokens --json`: time-series token/usage analytics.

## Required Capabilities
- `--group-by hour|day|week|month` (UTC buckets; weeks are ISO-8601 weeks, Mon-start)
- Filters (repeatable / comma-list):
  - `--agent <slug>`
  - `--workspace <path>` (resolved to workspace_id)
  - `--source <local|remote|all|source_id>`

## Metrics (v1)
Must be representable from rollups for O(#buckets) queries:
- Counts: `message_count`, `user_message_count`, `assistant_message_count`, `tool_call_count`, `plan_message_count`
- Content estimate: `content_tokens_est_total`, `content_tokens_est_user`, `content_tokens_est_assistant`
- API tokens: `api_tokens_total`, plus components (`api_input_tokens_total`, `api_output_tokens_total`, `api_cache_*`, `api_thinking_tokens_total`)
- Coverage: `api_coverage_message_count` and derived `api_coverage_pct`

## Derived Metrics (v1)
- `api_tokens_per_assistant_msg` (null if assistant_message_count=0)
- `content_tokens_per_user_msg` (null if user_message_count=0)
- `tool_calls_per_1k_tokens` (define denominator explicitly; include both:
  - per 1k API tokens (when api_tokens_total>0)
  - per 1k content-est tokens)
- Planning:
  - **v1**: `plan_message_pct = plan_message_count / message_count`
  - **v2** (z9fse.14): `plan_token_share` (token-attributed), either via new rollups or an explicit slow-path.

## Performance Contract
- Prefer rollups:
  - hour: `usage_hourly`
  - day/week/month: `usage_daily` (aggregate for week/month)
- If a requested slice cannot be served by rollups, return a structured slow-path indicator in `_meta` (and optionally require an explicit `--deep`).

## Output Contract
- Stable ordering by bucket key.
- stdout = JSON only; stderr = diagnostics.
- `_meta` must include `{ elapsed_ms, path: "rollup"|"slow", warning?: string }`.

## Testing & Logging
- Integration (z9fse.8): fixture spanning multiple days/hours; totals match across group-bys.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","title":"Task: CLI analytics validate (rollup invariants + drift detection)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics validate --json` as the correctness gate for analytics math and rollup integrity.

`status` tells us if data *looks* healthy; `validate` must prove key invariants actually hold.

## Scope
1. Implement fast invariant checks (default mode):
   - non-negative counters/tokens
   - required key columns present and non-null for aggregate keys
   - rollup rows exist for periods with source facts
2. Implement aggregate consistency checks:
   - sampled bucket sums in rollups equal recomputed sums from fact tables within tolerance
   - per-dimension totals reconcile with global totals
3. Implement cross-track drift checks:
   - Track A totals vs Track B totals are directionally consistent where overlap exists
   - identify stale/partial recomputation patterns
4. Provide two validation depths:
   - `--mode fast` (default): high-signal checks under ~1s on fixture DB
   - `--mode deep`: broader recomputation windows for CI/nightly confidence

## Output Contract
Return deterministic JSON with:
- `summary` (pass/fail + severity)
- `checks` array (id, status, measured, expected, tolerance, message)
- `drift_signals`
- `recommendations` (specific actions, not generic)
- `_meta` (elapsed_ms, mode, sample strategy)

## Performance + Safety
- Must be read-only and safe on live DB.
- Must fail closed: if a check cannot execute, report explicit `error` status rather than silently passing.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests per check function and tolerance logic.
- Integration tests with intentionally corrupted fixture datasets:
  - off-by-one rollup
  - negative token rows
  - stale track mismatch
- E2E tests:
  - validate success case returns `pass`
  - seeded failure case returns `fail` with actionable checks
- Logging on failure must include:
  - failed check id
  - measured vs expected values
  - bucket/dimension keys used in comparison","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics validate --json` emits stable JSON listing individual checks and an overall pass/fail summary.
2. Default mode runs quickly on fixture DB while still catching intentionally seeded corruption.
3. Deep mode increases confidence by expanding recomputation scope without changing check semantics.
4. Cross-track drift and rollup mismatch errors are clearly diagnosable from output alone.
5. Unit/integration/e2e coverage includes detailed logs for every failing check.","notes":"This command will be used as a preflight gate before trusting dashboards and before performance benchmarking runs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:28:01.827598307Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","performance","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","title":"Task: CLI analytics cost (USD estimates + pricing coverage)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics cost --json` for transparent USD estimation with explicit uncertainty and pricing coverage diagnostics.

Cost numbers are high-stakes; the command must never imply precision that the data does not support.

## Scope
1. Compute estimated cost from model pricing and token usage:
   - input/output/cache/thinking token components when available
   - explicit fallback rules when only partial token classes are present
2. Provide breakdowns by:
   - time bucket (day/week/month)
   - model family / model
   - agent/workspace/source filters
3. Emit pricing coverage quality:
   - % token volume matched to known pricing
   - list of unknown/unpriced models and affected token share
   - estimate-only warnings when API usage is sparse
4. Provide derived economics metrics:
   - cost per 1k API tokens
   - cost per assistant message
   - period-over-period delta

## Output Contract
Deterministic JSON sections:
- `filters`
- `pricing_coverage`
- `buckets`
- `breakdowns`
- `totals`
- `_meta`

Every monetary value should include explicit units (`usd`) and nullable semantics where computation is impossible.

## Precision / Safety Rules
- Use decimal-safe handling appropriate for currency.
- Avoid hidden rounding; round only for presentation layers while retaining raw numeric fields.
- Expose caveats in output when cost is partial.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - pricing rule matching
  - cost math across token classes
  - rounding and nullability behavior
- Integration tests:
  - mixed priced/unpriced model datasets
  - sparse API token coverage
  - period breakdown correctness
- E2E tests:
  - stable JSON schema
  - known fixture yields expected totals and warnings
- Logging on failure must include pricing match trace and unmatched-model diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics cost --json` reports USD estimates plus explicit pricing coverage diagnostics.
2. Unknown pricing and partial token coverage are surfaced clearly and never silently ignored.
3. Monetary calculations are reproducible and robust to sparse/partial inputs.
4. Time and dimension breakdowns are consistent with analytics query-library semantics.
5. Unit/integration/e2e tests with rich logs validate pricing mapping, math, and warning behavior.","notes":"Treat cost output as an analytics estimate with explicit confidence, not a billing ledger.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:36:07.287036785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.9","title":"Task: CLI analytics tools (per-tool breakdown + derived metrics)","description":"Add `cass analytics tools --json`: per-tool usage analytics that is stable for robots and fast for humans.

## What This Must Answer
- Which tools are invoked most?
- Tokens/cost associated with each tool (best-effort; coverage-aware)
- Trend over time (hour/day/week/month)

## Required Flags
- Time window: `--since/--until`, `--days N`, `--today`, `--week`
- Filters: `--agent`, `--workspace`, `--source`
- Grouping: `--group-by day|week|month` (hour optional; only if rollups exist)
- Top-N: `--limit N` (default 20) + explicit `other` bucket

## Metrics
At minimum (served from rollups created in z9fse.6):
- `invocation_count`
- `message_count_with_tool` (distinct messages that invoked the tool)
- `api_tokens_total` (and components when available)
- `content_tokens_est_total`
- `api_coverage_message_count`

Derived:
- `avg_api_tokens_per_invocation` (null if denom=0)
- `avg_content_tokens_per_invocation`
- `tool_calls_per_1k_tokens` (both api + content-est denominators)

## Output Contract (Robot-First)
- stdout = JSON only; stderr = diagnostics
- Stable ordering:
  - primarily by requested metric desc
  - tie-break by tool_name asc
- Include `_meta`: elapsed_ms, path=rollup|slow, warnings (coverage gaps)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Works on large corpora: uses tool rollups for O(#buckets + #tools) query time.
- Output is deterministic across runs on same DB.
- Coverage diagnostics are explicit (unknown tools/pricing never silently treated as 0).

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: query layer math (derived metrics) + stable sorting.
- Integration: fixture DB with known tool_use blocks -> CLI output matches expected.
- E2E scripts (z9fse.8): `cass analytics tools --json` smoke with stderr diagnostics captured.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:36:03.371812590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:29:42.983220872Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:36:03.371812590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:36:03.371812590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:36:03.371812590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
